3.2.1.requirement: Ensure that managers, systems administrators, and users of organizational information systems are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities and of the applicable policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of organizational information systems.
3.2.1.manual_guidance: Request security awareness training records and completion certificates. Verify training covers CUI handling, phishing, social engineering, and organizational security policies.

[a].text: security risks associated with organizational activities involving CUI are identified.

[b].text: policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of the system are identified.

[c].text: managers, systems administrators, and users of the system are made aware of the security risks associated with their activities.

[d].text: managers, systems administrators, and users of the system are made aware of the applicable policies, standards, and procedures related to the security of the system.

3.2.2.requirement: Ensure that organizational personnel are adequately trained to carry out their assigned information security-related duties and responsibilities.
3.2.2.manual_guidance: Request role-based training records for IT/security staff. Verify training covers specific duties such as incident response, system administration, and security monitoring.

[a].text: information security-related duties, roles, and responsibilities are defined.

[b].text: information security-related duties, roles, and responsibilities are assigned to designated personnel.

[c].text: personnel are adequately trained to carry out their assigned information security-related duties, roles, and responsibilities.

3.2.3.requirement: Provide security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat.

[b].text: security awareness training on recognizing and reporting potential indicators of insider threat is provided to managers and employees.

3.7.1.requirement: Perform maintenance on organizational information systems.

[a].text: system maintenance is performed.

3.7.2.requirement: Provide controls on the tools, techniques, mechanisms, and personnel used to conduct information system maintenance.
3.7.2.manual_guidance: Review maintenance tool inventory and authorization records. Verify that maintenance personnel are vetted and that maintenance tools are inspected and sanitized before use.

[a].text: tools used to conduct system maintenance are controlled.

[b].text: techniques used to conduct system maintenance are controlled.

[c].text: mechanisms used to conduct system maintenance are controlled.

[d].text: personnel used to conduct system maintenance are controlled.

3.7.3.requirement: Ensure equipment removed for off-site maintenance is sanitized of any CUI.
3.7.3.manual_guidance: Review media sanitization procedures for equipment sent off-site. Verify sanitization records exist for all equipment removed for maintenance.

[a].text: equipment to be removed from organizational spaces for off-site maintenance is sanitized of any CUI.

3.7.4.requirement: Check media containing diagnostic and test programs for malicious code before the media are used in organizational information systems.
3.7.4.manual_guidance: Review procedures for scanning maintenance media. Verify that anti-malware scans are performed on all diagnostic media before connection to production systems.

[a].text: media containing diagnostic and test programs are checked for malicious code before being used in organizational systems that process, store, or transmit CUI.

3.7.5.requirement: Require multifactor authentication to establish nonlocal maintenance sessions via external network connections and terminate such connections when nonlocal maintenance is complete.

[a].text: multifactor authentication is required to establish nonlocal maintenance sessions via external network connections.

3.7.6.requirement: Supervise the maintenance activities of maintenance personnel without required access authorization.

[a].text: maintenance personnel without required access authorization are supervised during maintenance activities.

3.8.1.requirement: Protect (i.e., physically control and securely store) information system media containing CUI, both paper and digital.
3.8.1.manual_guidance: Review media storage and handling procedures. Verify physical security controls for media storage areas and digital media access controls.

[a].text: paper media containing CUI is physically controlled.

[b].text: digital media containing CUI is physically controlled.

[c].text: paper media containing CUI is securely stored.

[d].text: digital media containing CUI is securely stored.

3.8.2.requirement: Limit access to CUI on information system media to authorized users.

[a].text: access to CUI on system media is limited to authorized users.

3.8.3.requirement: Sanitize or destroy information system media containing CUI before disposal or release for reuse.
3.8.3.manual_guidance: Review media sanitization procedures and records. Verify NIST 800-88 compliant sanitization methods are used. Check disposal vendor certifications and certificates of destruction.

[a].text: system media containing CUI is sanitized or destroyed before disposal.

[b].text: system media containing CUI is sanitized before it is released for reuse.

3.8.4.requirement: Mark media with necessary CUI markings and distribution limitations.

[a].text: media containing CUI is marked with applicable CUI markings.

[b].text: media containing CUI is marked with distribution limitations.

3.8.5.requirement: Control access to media containing CUI and maintain accountability for media during transport outside of controlled areas.
3.8.5.manual_guidance: Review media transport procedures and chain-of-custody records. Verify encryption requirements for media in transit and accountability tracking mechanisms.

[a].text: access to media containing CUI is controlled.

[b].text: accountability for media containing CUI is maintained during transport outside of controlled areas.

3.8.6.requirement: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to protect the confidentiality of CUI stored on digital media during transport unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards.

[a].text: the confidentiality of CUI stored on digital media is protected during transport using cryptographic mechanisms or alternative physical safeguards.

3.8.7.requirement: Control the use of removable media on information system components.
3.8.7.manual_guidance: Review removable media policies and endpoint DLP configurations. Verify USB device restrictions and authorized device whitelists.

[a].text: the use of removable media on system components containing CUI is controlled.

3.8.8.requirement: Prohibit the use of portable storage devices when such devices have no identifiable owner.
3.8.8.manual_guidance: Review portable storage device policies. Verify that all authorized portable storage devices are registered and assigned to specific individuals.

[a].text: the use of portable storage devices is prohibited when such devices have no identifiable owner.

3.8.9.requirement: Protect the confidentiality of backup CUI at storage locations.

[a].text: the confidentiality of backup CUI is protected at storage locations.

3.9.1.requirement: Screen individuals prior to authorizing access to organizational information systems containing CUI.
3.9.1.manual_guidance: Review personnel screening procedures and records. Verify background checks are completed before granting access to CUI systems. Check screening criteria meet organizational and regulatory requirements.

[a].text: individuals are screened prior to authorizing access to organizational systems.

3.9.2.requirement: Ensure that organizational information systems containing CUI are protected during and after personnel actions such as terminations and transfers.
3.9.2.manual_guidance: Review termination and transfer procedures. Verify that access is revoked promptly upon termination, credentials are disabled, and equipment/media are recovered. Check for automated deprovisioning workflows.

3.10.1.requirement: Limit physical access to organizational information systems, equipment, and the respective operating environments to authorized individuals.

3.10.2.requirement: Protect and monitor the physical facility and support infrastructure for organizational information systems.

3.10.3.requirement: Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity.

3.10.4.requirement: Maintain audit logs of physical access.

3.10.5.requirement: Control and manage physical access devices.
3.10.5.manual_guidance: Review inventory and management of physical access devices (keys, badges, combinations, PINs). Verify devices are inventoried, changed when compromised, and deactivated for separated personnel.

[a].text: physical access devices are identified.

3.10.6.requirement: Enforce safeguarding measures for CUI at alternate work sites.

[a].text: safeguarding measures for CUI are defined for alternate work sites.

3.11.1.requirement: Periodically assess the risk to organizational operations (including mission, functions, image, or reputation), organizational assets, and individuals, resulting from the operation of organizational information systems and the associated processing, storage, or transmission of CUI.
3.11.1.manual_guidance: Request the most recent risk assessment report. Verify it covers CUI systems, is updated at least annually, and addresses threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and impact.

3.11.2.requirement: Scan for vulnerabilities in organizational information systems and applications periodically and when new vulnerabilities affecting those systems and applications are identified.

[a].text: the frequency to scan for vulnerabilities in an organizational system and its applications that process, store, or transmit CUI is defined.

[b].text: vulnerability scans are performed in an organizational system that processes, stores, or transmits CUI with the defined frequency.

[c].text: vulnerability scans are performed in an application that contains CUI with the defined frequency.

[d].text: vulnerability scans are performed in an organizational system that processes, stores, or transmits CUI when new vulnerabilities are identified.

[e].text: vulnerability scans are performed in an application that contains CUI when new vulnerabilities are identified.

3.11.3.requirement: Remediate vulnerabilities in accordance with risk assessments.

[a].text: vulnerabilities are identified.

[b].text: vulnerabilities are remediated in accordance with risk assessments.

3.12.1.requirement: Periodically assess the security controls in organizational information systems to determine if the controls are effective in their application.
3.12.1.manual_guidance: Request security assessment reports. Verify assessments are conducted at least annually, cover all security controls, and identify control effectiveness with findings and recommendations.

[a].text: the frequency of security control assessments is defined.

[b].text: security controls are assessed with the defined frequency to determine if the controls are effective in their application.

3.12.2.requirement: Develop and implement plans of action designed to correct deficiencies and reduce or eliminate vulnerabilities in organizational information systems.
3.12.2.manual_guidance: Request the Plan of Action and Milestones (POA&M). Verify it documents known deficiencies, planned corrective actions, responsible parties, and target completion dates.

[a].text: deficiencies and vulnerabilities to be addressed by the plan of action are identified.

[b].text: a plan of action is developed to correct identified deficiencies and reduce or eliminate identified vulnerabilities.

[c].text: the plan of action is implemented to correct identified deficiencies and reduce or eliminate identified vulnerabilities.

3.12.3.requirement: Monitor security controls on an ongoing basis to ensure the continued effectiveness of the controls.

[a].text: security controls are monitored on an ongoing basis to ensure the continued effectiveness of those controls.

3.12.4.requirement: Develop, document, and periodically update system security plans that describe system boundaries, system environments of operation, how security requirements are implemented, and the relationships with or connections to other systems.
3.12.4.manual_guidance: Request the System Security Plan (SSP). Verify it documents system boundaries, operating environment, security control implementation, and interconnections. Check for annual review and updates.

[a].text: a system security plan is developed.

[b].text: the system boundary is described and documented in the system security plan.

[c].text: the system environment of operation is described and documented in the system security plan.

[d].text: the security requirements identified and approved by the designated authority as non-applicable are identified.

[e].text: the method of security requirement implementation is described and documented in the system security plan.

[f].text: the relationship with or connection to other systems is described and documented in the system security plan.

[g].text: the frequency to update the system security plan is defined.

3.13.1.requirement: Monitor, control, and protect organizational communications (i.e., information transmitted or received by organizational information systems) at the external boundaries and key internal boundaries of the information systems.

[a].text: the external system boundary is defined.

[b].text: key internal system boundaries are defined.

[c].text: communications are monitored at the external system boundary.

[d].text: communications are monitored at key internal boundaries.

[e].text: communications are controlled at the external system boundary.

[f].text: communications are controlled at key internal boundaries.

[g].text: communications are protected at the external system boundary.

[h].text: communications are protected at key internal boundaries.

3.13.2.requirement: Employ architectural designs, software development techniques, and systems engineering principles that promote effective information security within organizational information systems.
3.13.2.manual_guidance: Review system architecture documentation. Verify defense-in-depth design, network segmentation, and secure development practices are documented and implemented.

[a].text: architectural designs that promote effective information security are identified.

[b].text: software development techniques that promote effective information security are identified.

[c].text: systems engineering principles that promote effective information security are identified.

[d].text: identified architectural designs that promote effective information security are employed.

[e].text: identified software development techniques that promote effective information security are employed.

[f].text: identified systems engineering principles that promote effective information security are employed.

3.13.3.requirement: Separate user functionality from information system management functionality.

[a].text: user functionality is identified.

[b].text: system management functionality is identified.

[c].text: user functionality is separated from system management functionality.

3.13.4.requirement: Prevent unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources.

[a].text: unauthorized and unintended information transfer via shared system resources is prevented.

3.13.5.requirement: Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components that are physically or logically separated from internal networks.

[a].text: publicly accessible system components are identified.

[b].text: subnetworks for publicly accessible system components are physically or logically separated from internal networks.

3.13.6.requirement: Deny network communications traffic by default and allow network communications traffic by exception (i.e., deny all, permit by exception).

[a].text: network communications traffic is denied by default.

[b].text: network communications traffic is allowed by exception.

3.13.7.requirement: Prevent remote devices from simultaneously establishing non-remote connections with organizational information systems and communicating via some other connection to resources in external networks (i.e., split tunneling).

[a].text: remote devices are prevented from simultaneously establishing non-remote connections with the system and communicating via some other connection to resources in external networks (i.e., split tunneling).

3.13.8.requirement: Implement cryptographic mechanisms to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission unless otherwise protected by alternative physical safeguards.

[a].text: cryptographic mechanisms intended to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI are identified.

[b].text: alternative physical safeguards intended to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI are identified.

[c].text: either cryptographic mechanisms or alternative physical safeguards are implemented to prevent unauthorized disclosure of CUI during transmission.

3.13.9.requirement: Terminate network connections associated with communications sessions at the end of the sessions or after a defined period of inactivity.

[a].text: a period of inactivity to terminate network connections associated with communications sessions is defined.

[b].text: network connections associated with communications sessions are terminated at the end of the sessions.

[c].text: network connections associated with communications sessions are terminated after the defined period of inactivity.

3.13.10.requirement: Establish and manage cryptographic keys for cryptography employed in organizational information systems.

[a].text: cryptographic keys are established whenever cryptography is employed.

[b].text: cryptographic keys are managed whenever cryptography is employed.

3.13.11.requirement: Employ FIPS-validated cryptography when used to protect the confidentiality of CUI.

[a].text: FIPS-validated cryptography is employed to protect the confidentiality of CUI.

3.13.12.requirement: Prohibit remote activation of collaborative computing devices and provide indication of devices in use to users present at the device.
3.13.12.manual_guidance: Review policies for collaborative computing devices (cameras, microphones, conferencing systems). Verify remote activation is disabled and indicators of active use are enabled.

[a].text: collaborative computing devices are identified.

[b].text: collaborative computing devices provide indication to users of devices in use.

[c].text: remote activation of collaborative computing devices is prohibited.

3.13.13.requirement: Control and monitor the use of mobile code.

[a].text: use of mobile code is controlled.

[b].text: use of mobile code is monitored.

3.13.14.requirement: Control and monitor the use of Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies.
3.13.14.manual_guidance: Review VoIP security policies and configurations. Verify VoIP traffic is encrypted, segregated on dedicated VLANs, and monitored for anomalous activity.

[a].text: use of Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies is controlled.

[b].text: use of Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) technologies is monitored.

3.13.15.requirement: Protect the authenticity of communications sessions.

[a].text: the authenticity of communications sessions is protected.

3.13.16.requirement: Protect the confidentiality of CUI at rest.

[a].text: the confidentiality of CUI at rest is protected.

3.14.1.requirement: Identify, report, and correct information and information system flaws in a timely manner.

[a].text: the time within which to identify system flaws is specified.

[b].text: system flaws are identified within the specified time frame.

[c].text: the time within which to report system flaws is specified.

[d].text: system flaws are reported within the specified time frame.

[e].text: the time within which to correct system flaws is specified.

[f].text: system flaws are corrected within the specified time frame.

3.14.2.requirement: Provide protection from malicious code at appropriate locations within organizational information systems.

[a].text: designated locations for malicious code protection are identified.

[b].text: protection from malicious code at designated locations is provided.

3.14.3.requirement: Monitor information system security alerts and advisories and take action in response.

[a].text: response actions to system security alerts and advisories are identified.

[b].text: system security alerts and advisories are monitored.

[c].text: actions in response to system security alerts and advisories are taken.

3.14.4.requirement: Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available.

[a].text: malicious code protection mechanisms are updated when new releases are available.

3.14.5.requirement: Perform periodic scans of the information system and real-time scans of files from external sources as files are downloaded, opened, or executed.

[a].text: the frequency for malicious code scans is defined.

[b].text: malicious code scans are performed with the defined frequency.

[c].text: real-time malicious code scans of files from external sources as files are downloaded, opened, or executed are performed.

3.14.6.requirement: Monitor organizational information systems, including inbound and outbound communications traffic, to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

[a].text: the system is monitored to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

[b].text: inbound communications traffic is monitored to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

[c].text: outbound communications traffic is monitored to detect attacks and indicators of potential attacks.

3.14.7.requirement: Identify unauthorized use of organizational information systems.

[a].text: authorized use of the system is defined.

[b].text: unauthorized use of the system is identified.

far_practices: b.1.i - Limit information system access to authorized users
b.1.ii - Limit information system access to authorized functions
b.1.iii - Verify and control/limit connections to external systems
b.1.iv - Control information posted or processed on publicly accessible systems
b.1.v - Identify information system users, processes, or devices
b.1.vi - Authenticate identities before allowing access
b.1.vii - Sanitize or destroy information system media before disposal or reuse
b.1.viii - Limit physical access to organizational information systems
b.1.ix - Escort visitors and monitor visitor activity
b.1.x - Maintain audit logs of physical access
b.1.xi - Control and manage physical access devices
b.1.xii - Monitor, control, and protect organizational communications at external boundaries
b.1.xiii - Implement subnetworks for publicly accessible system components
b.1.xiv - Identify, report, and correct information and information system flaws
b.1.xv - Provide protection from malicious code at appropriate locations
b.1.xvi - Update malicious code protection mechanisms when new releases are available
b.1.xvii - Perform periodic scans of the information system and real-time scans of files from external sources
